summary: Strong, fully parseable triple-AND of three R2 top-10 components; sample size adequate at the conservative end and structurally distinct from any single-component existing archetype.

analyses.distinctness_notes: E126 is taker_buy_extreme alone, E064 is taker_buy_dominant alone, E051 is high_liquidity alone, E148 is global_short_high alone — all on pricechange/long. This proposal is the first triple intersection of taker_buy_ratio_5m>=0.65 ∧ liquidity_bucket=high ∧ global_short_ratio_high, materially narrower than any single-component archetype and not equivalent to any pairwise combo in registry.
analyses.triggers_estimate_method: 5000 pricechange events × ~0.50 pump bias on long side baseline ≈ 2500 long-eligible. Apply taker_buy_ratio_5m>=0.65 cut (~0.40 of pump-side, by analogy to E126 trig=3443 / E064 trig=3619 representing looser thresholds; 0.65 is stricter, ~0.30 retained) ≈ 750. Apply liquidity_bucket=high (~0.40 of universe per E051 trig=2157/5000) → ~300. Apply global_short_ratio_high (p75 flag, ~0.25 of remaining, weakly correlated with the others) → ~75–150. Take upper end given mild positive correlation between taker-buy aggression and short-skew sentiment → 150.
analyses.exit_compatibility_notes: Coverage summary shows fixed dominates positive scores (6680/7320 positive, max 0.395) while multi_tp/trail/time_only/breakeven are entirely non-positive in this dataset. A selective high-conviction long entry like this should pair best with fixed (tight TP, modest SL to avoid the asymmetric TP/SL trap flagged in R2 E126 paper-sim) and secondarily breakeven for tail protection. Recommend GPU sweep prioritize fixed family with TP/SL ratios that survive kelly_capped and p25_capped_tail metrics rather than legacy p25.
